items.title: Exclusive-Nvidia begins Vera CPU sales pitch to Chinese clients, sources say
items.summary: Nvidia has told Chinese clients that its new "Vera" central processors for AI data centres could be available as soon as August and that they can begin placing orders, three sources familiar with the matter said.  Nvidia's market share in China has effectively fallen to zero, its CEO Jensen Huang said in October, hurt by U.S. export controls on advanced ​chips and Beijing's push for self-reliance in key technologies.  The move also raises the stakes in its competition with major CPU firms Intel and AMD, which are racing to increase supplies of server CPUs for AI data centres.
